terms.definition: A rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns, often used to represent linear transformations or structured data like datasets and images.
A matrix with the same number of rows and columns (n x n), important because it can have special properties like determinants, eigenvalues and inverses.
A square matrix with 1 on the main diagonal and 0 elsewhere; it acts as the multiplicative identity for matrix multiplication of the same size.
A square matrix whose off-diagonal entries are all zero; only the main diagonal entries may be non-zero.
A square matrix that is equal to its transpose, so a_ij = a_ji for all i and j.
The operation that swaps rows and columns of a matrix, producing a new matrix A^T with entries (A^T)_ij = A_ji.
An operation that combines two matrices of the same shape into a new matrix by adding corresponding entries.
An operation where every entry of a matrix is multiplied by the same scalar value.
